# ADR-260038: Network View Target-Attach Activation and Selected-State Ring Visibility Refinement

## Workflow

- This ADR records a post-BI-260038/BI-260039 refinement for Network-view connection interactions.
- It separates remaining corrective compliance from newly requested interaction behavior.
- It supersedes the selected-state ring visibility rule from ADR-260036 while preserving outer-ring initiation grammar.

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-14
Owners: Repository owner, product collaborator
Supersedes: ADR-260036 (D2 only)

## Context

Latest validation feedback identified three remaining expectations:

1. During drag, target magnetic attach should activate only when pointer is on top of the target atom hit area (atom disk including outer ring), not in a larger proximity zone.
2. During drag, preview endpoint snap should follow nearest-boundary geometry rather than fixed-side snap points.
3. Outer initiation ring should not remain visible solely because the node is selected; selected state should be represented by inner selection visual only.

Additionally, connection completion must reliably succeed when releasing over any point of the target atom disk and outer ring, including center release.

## Decision

### D1: Selected-State Visibility Rule Refinement
- Outer initiation ring is visible on hover and active connection drag affordance states.
- Outer initiation ring is not shown solely due to selected state.
- Selected state remains represented by inner selection visual semantics.

### D2: Magnetic Attach Activation Boundary
- Target magnetic attach activates only when pointer overlap is within the target atom acceptance area.
- Acceptance area includes full atom disk and outer ring.
- Near-but-outside proximity must not activate target attach.

### D3: Drag-Time Snap Geometry
- When target attach is active, preview endpoint snap uses nearest-boundary geometry derived from source-target direction.
- Fixed four-side snap behavior is not the target baseline for drag preview.

### D4: Completion Reliability Guard
- Releasing over any point inside target disk or outer ring must complete connection flow.
- Releasing on blank canvas or source atom remains no-op.

### D5: Scope Boundary
- This refinement applies to Network view only.
- Flow-view interaction model remains unchanged.

## Alternatives Considered

- Option A: Keep selected-state outer ring visibility for parity with prior rule. Rejected: conflicts with clarified interaction intent.
- Option B: Keep proximity-based magnetic attach. Rejected: creates premature snapping and ambiguous targeting.
- Option C: Keep fixed-side drag snap points. Rejected: less spatially coherent than nearest-boundary geometry.

## Consequences

### Positive
- Reduces accidental attach and improves targeting predictability.
- Aligns drag-time visual behavior with established edge-geometry expectations.
- Clarifies visual semantics between initiation affordance and selection state.

### Negative
- Requires more precise hit-testing and interaction test coverage.

### Risks
- Hit-area math inconsistencies could reintroduce center-drop failures.
- Renderer internals may constrain exact nearest-boundary preview endpoint behavior.

## Verification

- Verify selected node does not show outer ring unless hovered/active-drag state applies.
- Verify magnetic attach does not trigger outside target disk+ring area.
- Verify drag preview snap endpoint follows nearest-boundary geometry.
- Verify center release on target atom completes connection.
- Verify blank/source release remains no-op.

## Rollback

- If nearest-boundary drag preview cannot be reliably supported, retain strict activation boundary and completion reliability while documenting a temporary snap fallback.
